_notes.source: Representative IEEE C57.91-2011 values for a 50 kVA ONAN distribution/service transformer. Exponents from IEEE Table 4. Rated thermal parameters are typical for a 65 °C-rise class ONAN distribution transformer designed to reach Theta_H = 110 °C at K=1, Theta_A = 30 °C: Theta_A(30) + Delta_Theta_TO,R(55) + Delta_Theta_H,R(25) = 110 °C. These are NOT from a specific IEEE C57.91 worked example.
_notes.cooling_type_note: ONAN: Oil Natural Air Natural. Passive natural convection — no pumps or fans. Cooling exponents n=0.8 and m=0.8 from IEEE C57.91-2011 Table 4.
_notes.normal_life_hours: 180000 hours (IEEE C57.91 Table 1, thermally upgraded kraft paper insulation)
_notes.reference_hot_spot_temp_c: 110 °C is the reference temperature for normal aging rate per IEEE C57.91 Section 7. Calibrated for ONAN transformers at rated load (K=1) in 30 °C ambient.
_notes.time_constant_note: tau_o = 0.75 h. A 50 kVA unit has roughly one-tenth the oil volume of a 500 kVA unit. IEEE C57.91 Eq. 14: tau_TO,R = C * Delta_Theta_TO,R / P_T,R. Smaller C and lower P_T,R both compress the time constant; typical published range for 25-100 kVA ONAN units is 0.5-1.0 h. Effective time constant = k11 * tau_o = 0.5 * 0.75 = 0.375 h.

name: ieee_service_transformer_50kva
_notes_top_oil_rise: Delta_Theta_TO,R = 55 °C: rated top-oil rise over ambient at full load. Same design target as the 500 kVA unit — the 65 °C-rise class defines this irrespective of size.
_notes_hot_spot_rise: Delta_Theta_H,R = 25 °C: rated hot-spot rise over top-oil at full load. Together with top_oil_rise_c: Theta_H,SS = 30 + 55 + 25 = 110 °C at K=1, the IEEE reference design point for ONAN (F_AA = 1.0 at rated load).
_notes_thermal_time_constant: tau_o = 0.75 h: oil thermal time constant. Shorter than the 500 kVA unit (1.5 h) because a 50 kVA transformer has much less oil volume (thermal mass C) while losses scale proportionally with kVA. IEEE C57.91 Eq. 14: tau_TO,R = C * Delta_Theta_TO,R / P_T,R. Effective time constant = k11 * tau_o = 0.5 * 0.75 = 0.375 h.
_notes_winding_time_constant: tau_w = 0.033 h (2 min): winding thermal time constant. Shorter than the 500 kVA unit (0.067 h) due to smaller winding mass. At this size the winding gradient is nearly instantaneous for any dt >= 1 min.
_notes_oil_exponent: n = 0.8 for ONAN (IEEE C57.91-2011 Table 4). k11 = 0.5 corrects the linearised time-constant model for the nonlinear ONAN cooling response (n < 1).
_notes_winding_exponent: y = 2m = 1.6 for ONAN (IEEE C57.91-2011 Table 4: m = 0.8). Controls how the hot-spot gradient scales with load: Delta_Theta_H = Delta_Theta_H,R * K^1.6.
_notes_load_loss_ratio: R = 4.0: ratio of load loss to no-load loss at rated current. Lower than the 500 kVA unit (R=5.0) because no-load core losses are proportionally larger in very small transformers — the core must be sized for voltage regulation regardless of kVA rating.
_notes_hot_spot_factor: H = 1.0: hot-spot factor, standard multiplier on the hot-spot gradient.
_notes_k_constants: k11 = 0.5: required for all ONAN cooling modes (n < 1) per IEEE C57.91 Section 7.2.1. k21 = 1.0, k22 = 1.0: IEEE C57.91 Annex C simplified single-component winding model.
_notes_limits: Normal loading limits per IEEE C57.91-2011 Table 3 (thermally upgraded insulation).
